initial_program:
% Determine the number of rows and columns
numRows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
numCols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Maximum row index (last row) and maximum column index (last column)
RowMax(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max {R : input(R, _, _)}.
ColMax(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max {C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Wall colour  the highest colour that appears in the grid
Wall(W) :- W = #max {C : input(_, _, C)}.

% 4connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Boundary cells (border and not a wall cell)
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, R = Rmax.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, C = Cmax.

% Exterior reachable cells  expand only through zero cells
reachable(R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,0).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC,
                    input(R,C,0).

% Exactly one output per cell
% Wall cells keep their colour.
cell(R,C,W) :- input(R,C,V), V = W.
% Exterior (outside) nonwall cells become 0.
cell(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, reachable(R,C).
% Interior (inside) nonwall cells keep their original colour.
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, not reachable(R,C), Color = V.

% Produce exactly one output for every input cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C, Color).

rounds.program_after:
% Determine the number of rows and columns
numRows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
numCols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Maximum row index (last row) and maximum column index (last column)
RowMax(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max {R : input(R, _, _)}.
ColMax(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max {C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Wall colour  the highest colour that appears in the grid
Wall(W) :- W = #max {C : input(_, _, C)}.

% 4connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Boundary cells (border and not a wall cell)
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, R = Rmax.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, C = Cmax.

% Exterior reachable cells  expand only through zero cells
reachable(R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,0).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC,
                    input(R,C,0).

% Exactly one output per cell
% Wall cells keep their colour.
cell(R,C,W) :- input(R,C,V), V = W.
% Exterior (outside) nonwall cells become 0.
cell(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, reachable(R,C).
% Interior (inside) nonwall cells keep their original colour.
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, not reachable(R,C), Color = V.

% Produce exactly one output for every input cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C, Color).

rounds.program_after:
% Determine the number of rows and columns
numRows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
numCols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Maximum row index (last row) and maximum column index (last column)
RowMax(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
ColMax(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colour  the highest colour that appears in the grid
Wall(W) :- W = #max {C : input(_, _, C)}.

% 4connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Boundary cells (border and not a wall cell)
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, R = Rmax.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, C = Cmax.

% Exterior reachable cells  expand only through zero cells
reachable(R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,0).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC,
                    input(R,C,0).

% Exactly one output per cell
% Wall cells keep their colour.
cell(R,C,W) :- input(R,C,V), V = W.
% Exterior (outside) nonwall cells become 0.
cell(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, reachable(R,C).
% Interior (inside) nonwall cells keep their original colour.
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, not reachable(R,C), Color = V.

% Produce exactly one output for every input cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C, Color).

final_program:
% Determine the number of rows and columns
numRows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
numCols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Maximum row index (last row) and maximum column index (last column)
RowMax(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
ColMax(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colour  the highest colour that appears in the grid
Wall(W) :- W = #max {C : input(_, _, C)}.

% 4connected neighbourhood offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Boundary cells (border and not a wall cell)
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, R = Rmax.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, C = Cmax.

% Exterior reachable cells  expand only through zero cells
reachable(R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,0).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC,
                    input(R,C,0).

% Exactly one output per cell
% Wall cells keep their colour.
cell(R,C,W) :- input(R,C,V), V = W.
% Exterior (outside) nonwall cells become 0.
cell(R,C,0) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, reachable(R,C).
% Interior (inside) nonwall cells keep their original colour.
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != W, not reachable(R,C), Color = V.

% Produce exactly one output for every input cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C, Color).
